title: Fix deprecation warning / error from BioPython
body:
Hi,

   Here is a small PR to fix a deprecation issue related to BioPython. It would be great if y'all could run this through CI to make sure it doesn't have any side effects, but BioPython _seems_ to promise it's a safe change.

## Description:
This PR swaps the BioPython.SCOPData module import in the mmcif_parsing module for BioPython.PDBData.

## Related issue:
See https://github.com/biopython/biopython/blob/master/DEPRECATED.rst#biodatascopdata for information about the SCOPData deprecation.

## Motivation:
SCOPData was removed in version 1.82 of BioPython, so its usage throws an error when imported. This change allows upgrading BioPython beyond version 1.79, which brings improvements in security and stability. I had been trying to use AlphaFold2 with an updated BioPython but kept receiving deprecation warnings / errors depending on which version I was using.

## Verification
  [X] Package still installs after change
  [X] `run_alphafold_test.py` returns `ok` after this change.

step_plan.summary: Update mmCIF parsing to stop importing the removed `Bio.Data.SCOPData` module and use Biopython’s supported `Bio.Data.PDBData` mapping for 3-letter to 1-letter residue conversion, preserving the existing `'X'` fallback behavior.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated SCOPData import with PDBData (optionally via a small compatibility shim) and update 3-letter→1-letter residue translation inside parsing to preserve existing fallback behavior
steps.reason: Biopython removed `Bio.Data.SCOPData` in v1.82; `parse()` currently relies on `SCOPData.protein_letters_3to1` to build per-chain SEQRES strings. Switching to `Bio.Data.PDBData` (or a guarded import that prefers it) eliminates the import error while keeping the same mapping semantics and the existing `'X'` fallback for unknown/unsupported residue codes.
steps.side_effects: Changes the dependency surface on Biopython’s data module; if `PDBData` exposes a different mapping name/shape in some Biopython versions, parsing could regress unless guarded. Downstream effects would show up in any code paths consuming `MmcifObject.chain_to_seqres` / template features; run `run_alphafold_test.py` and any template/mmCIF-related tests in CI to confirm identical outputs for standard residues.
